title: move bachelorprojekt schema to website DB, fix timeline API
description:
## Summary
- `bachelorprojekt` schema was being created in the `postgres` DB instead of `website` DB — the website API's `trackingPool` had no valid DB URL and threw `TRACKING_DB_URL not set` silently, causing the Kore timeline to return nothing
- `features` table and `v_timeline` view were missing from the schema script entirely (only existed in `postgres` DB from a prior manual migration)
- `TRACKING_DB_URL` in both env secrets pointed to `postgres` DB as superuser; now targets `website` DB as `website` user

## Changes
- **`k3d/website-schema.yaml`**: `ensure-bachelorprojekt-schema.sh` now targets `--dbname website`; adds `features` table, `v_timeline` view, and grants for the `website` role
- **`website/src/lib/website-db.ts`**: `trackingPool` falls back to `SESSIONS_DATABASE_URL` (already in pod env) so no new secret injection needed; adds `nodeLookup` for DNS consistency
- **`environments/.secrets/`**: `TRACKING_DB_URL` updated to `website` DB for both envs; re-sealed

## Test plan
- [ ] `bachelorprojekt.v_timeline` returns 461 rows in `website` DB on mentolder
- [ ] `/api/timeline` on `web.korczewski.de` returns entries
- [ ] `tracking-import` CronJob writes new PR entries to `website.bachelorprojekt.features`
- [ ] E2E website tests pass on korczewski

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
